channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Panthers' Sergei Bobrovsky gets pulled after allowing 5 goals in Stanley Cup Final Game 4
item.description: EDMONTON, Alberta (AP) — Paul Maurice had seen enough after Sergei Bobrovsky allowed his fifth goal in Game 4 of the Stanley Cup Final, deciding to pull the Florida Panthers goaltender who was the biggest reason the team is one win from a championship.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greene homers twice and has a career-best 6 RBIs as Tigers rout Astros 13-5
item.description: Riley Greene homered twice and set career highs with four hits and six RBIs as the Detroit Tigers raced to a huge lead and cruised to a 13-5 win over the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: Local 4

item.title: DeChambeau a one-man show at Pinehurst No. 2 and leads US Open by 3
item.description: PINEHURST, N.C. (AP) — Bryson DeChambeau ducked outside the ropes to a private area in the woods, flat on his back as trainers worked on hips that felt too tight. Moments later, he unleashed a swing that sent his drive 347 yards, leaving a wedge to set up birdie and another fist pump.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA champion Paul Pierce weighs in on decision to leave Caitlin Clark off US Olympic team: 'It was a bad move'
item.description: Ex-NBA star Paul Pierce directed some harsh criticism toward USA Basketball officials after their decision to leave Caitlin Clark off the Olympics roster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Near-identical triple bogeys by Tony Finau and Ludvig Aberg minutes apart derail their US Open hopes
item.description: PINEHURST, N.C. (AP) — Tony Finau and Ludvig Aberg had their U.S. Open dreams dealt a major dent when they ping-ponged their way around the exact same green about 30 minutes apart Saturday during a third round played in oppressive heat at Pinehurst No. 2.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gretchen Walsh sets a world record in the 100-meter butterfly at the U.S. Olympic trials
item.description: Gretchen Walsh set a world record in the women’s 100-meter butterfly, posting a time of 55.18 seconds in a semifinal heat at the U.S. Olympic swimming trials.
item.dc:creator: Paul Newberry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Olympic gold medalist Katie Ledecky is an ‘incredible leader for Team USA,’ swim legend Missy Franklin says
item.description: Five-time Olympic gold medalist Missy Franklin spoke with Fox News Digital about her expectations for Katie Ledecky ahead of the 2024 Summer Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Martinez homers twice, Mets beat Padres 5-1
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — J.D. Martinez homered twice, Jose Quintana ended a 10-start winless streak and the New York Mets beat the San Diego Padres 5-1 Saturday for their eighth win in 10 games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Redbird Summer League: SJA 63 Norseman 36
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Summer League hoops in the Redbird Summer League with SJA beating the Norsemen 63-36 at Hoover High.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Scheffler has another tough day fighting the Pinehurst No. 2 greens at the US Open
item.description: World top-ranked player Scottie Scheffler closed play Saturday at the U.S. Open with his third straight over-par round.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy concedes goal after 23 seconds but recovers to beat Albania 2-1 at Euro 2024
item.description: Italy has recovered from conceding the quickest goal in European Championship history after just 23 seconds to open its title defense with a 2-1 win over Albania.
item.dc:creator: Steve Douglas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SDSU Passing League: Cathedral Catholic 32, Chino Hills 15
item.description: The Cathedral Catholic Dons are back in action on the football field, as they defeat Chino Hills 32-15 at SDSU's Passing League.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Bay Area water polo legend Maggie Steffens seeks fourth gold at Paris Olympics
item.description: Prolific goal scorer Maggie Steffens, a former Stanford and Monte Vista High star, might already be the best ever. Just don't mention it to her.
item.dc:creator: Jason Mastrodonato, The Mercury News

item.title: Charles Barkley says next season will be his last on TV, no matter what happens with NBA media deals
item.description: Charles Barkley has spent the last 24 years working as a studio analyst for TNT, which could lose the rights to broadcast NBA after next season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Joey Chestnut
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Let Joey eat. When you think of the Fourth of July, you think of a celebration of America, patriotism, summer, the American flag, Neil Diamond, the beach, cookouts, fireworks and, of course, Coney Island and the Nathan’s Famous Hot Dog Eating Contest.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: How CMU's Olvia Cummins made the Olympics
item.description: Hear from Colorado Mesa's first active student-athlete to compete on the world's biggest stage in the video below.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: MLB investigates, disciplines umpire for gambling
item.description: NEW YORK — Major League Baseball intends to discipline umpire Pat Hoberg for gambling, according to a statement provided by MLB's league office.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls fans can bid on iconic Jordan 1s from 'Broken Foot Game'
item.description: *A link to the auction site where these items are being sold can be found at the bottom of this article.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Houston Astros release former White Sox MVP, all-star Jose Abreu
item.description: HOUSTON — The Houston Astros have released first baseman Jose Abreu from the Major League roster, according to a post from the team on X. The move was announced by Astro's general manager Dana Brown Friday afternoon, with a corresponding roster move expected later in the day.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Fresno State Athletics gets an official craft beer partner?
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE/KGPE) - Fresno State Athletics now has an official craft beer partner as part of a first-of-its-kind sponsorship. The college announced on Friday that Tioga Sequoia Brewing Company has signed on to be the craft beer partner of Fresno State Athletics.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Cam Rising hosts football camp for kids in Herriman
item.description: HERRIMAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Utes quarterback Cam Rising wasn't going to let the hot temperatures stop his football camp Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bucs' rookies staying in the heat to prepare for training camp, upcoming season
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- The Tampa Bay Buccaneers officially end mandatory minicamp Thursday and are off until late July, but a few rookies plan on staying in the heat to prepare for training camp. "Be ready for the heat," running back Bucky Irving said.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Jose Sharks hire new head coach
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — The San Jose Sharks have promoted Ryan Warsofsky to become the team's new head coach after two years as an assistant under the recently fired David Quinn. General manager Mike Grier announced Thursday that Warsofsky will become the 11th head coach in franchise history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tom Brady enshrined into Patriots Hall of Fame during star-studded night
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — Tom Brady brought the New England Patriots endless memories and six Super Bowl trophies during his two-decade run with the team. The Patriots made sure his induction into the franchise's Hall of Fame was a celebration unlike it had ever thrown before.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Next man up: Lakers reportedly set to formally interview another head coaching candidate
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers' search for their next head coach has already been a roller coaster ride, but now the illustrious franchise seems to be taking a large step with an oft-rumored candidate.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Jimmer Fredette excited to play in the Olympics
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Jimmer Fredette is an Olympic fanatic. "I love the Olympics," the former BYU star said. "Like, it's one of my favorite things. I watch every event and I have all the way up until now." Now, he gets to be a part of the Summer Games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Giants sign Oakland Ballers pitcher Elijah Pleasants
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- A pitcher for the Oakland Ballers will be crossing the bay to pitch for the San Francisco Giants' organization. The Ballers announced Wednesday that righty Elijah Pleasants' contract was purchased by the Giants.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Remembering Jerry West
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- "The Logo." "Mr. Clutch." "Mr. Outside." "Zeke from Cabin Creek." There were many nicknames for Jerry West over his playing career. He was an icon. West passed away Wednesday at the age of 86.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Lawyer asks to auction off OJ Simpson's personal items
item.description: Simpson died on April 11 at the age of 76. His long-time attorney, Malcolm LaVergne, was tasked to handle his trust and estate.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Magic Johnson remembers 'great friend and confidant' Jerry West
item.description: Magic Johnson said he was "devastated" to learn of Jerry West's passing. The Lakers legend paid tribute to the team's former General Manager and NBA icon. In a lengthy post to Instagram, the five-time NBA champion remembered when he first met West.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Michael Jordan, Shaq, Lebron James remember Jerry West
item.description: The sports world and the city of Los Angeles are mourning the loss of NBA legend Jerry West. Michael Jordan issued a statement and said he was "deeply saddened" by the news of West's death. "He was truly a friend and mentor- like an older brother to me.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Tom Brady enshrined into Patriots Hall of Fame
item.description: The New England Patriots paid tribute Wednesday night to an already legendary figure in team history: Tom Brady.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Begley, NewsNation

item.title: Henry Winkler evacuated after Ireland hotel catches fire
item.description: Henry Winkler was awoken by an emergency while in Dublin, Ireland on Wednesday morning. The actor and many others within Dublin's historic Shelbourne Hotel were ordered to evacuate after a fire broke out. Regardless of the emergency, The Fonz was in good spirits.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Travis Kelce begs for high workload heading into 12th season
item.description: Movie deals, one of the world's most influential relationships, and a popular podcast fade away when Travis Kelce hits the football field.
item.dc:creator: PJ Green, NewsNation

item.title: Jerry West, a 3-time Hall of Fame selection and the NBA logo, dies at 86
item.description: (AP) -- Jerry West, who was selected to the Basketball Hall of Fame three times in a storied career as a player and executive and whose silhouette is considered to be the basis of the NBA logo, died Wednesday morning, the Los Angeles Clippers announced. He was 86. West, nicknamed “Mr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Rodgers misses mandatory minicamp
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There are things to worry about in life and in sports. Aaron Rodgers missing mandatory minicamp for the New York Jets is not that big of a deal.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Walberg adds two transfer guards
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. - New Fresno State men's basketball head coach Vance Walberg continues to build his roster for next season. Monday, David Douglas Jr., the Freshman of the Year last season in the Horizon League, was officially signed by the Bulldogs. Douglas Jr.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bemis, KSEE, CBS
